curator: auto (generate_day.py — reals verified verbatim vs source + cross-validated)

quotes.text: I used to do drugs. I still do, but I used to, too.
quotes.speaker: Mitch Hedberg
quotes.context: One of late absurdist comedian Mitch Hedberg's most iconic one-liners, a paradoxical drug-use confession from his album 'Strategic Grill Locations' (1999). Hedberg's easygoing stoner persona and drug jokes were central to his act.

quotes.text: Maybe it was because we did it unabashedly out in the open. We didn't know it was shocking. Perhaps we were just too fucking frank.
quotes.speaker: Slash
quotes.context: The Guns N' Roses guitarist, in a Classic Rock/Louder interview, explaining why his band got a wilder reputation than peers who did 'the same shit'.

source.title: Louder (Classic Rock) — Six Pack: Slash on drugs, dying and Axl Rose

quotes.text: Somebody asked me once what the secret to a long marriage was. I said, 'Separate bathrooms and a very short memory.' They laughed. I wasn't joking, you nosy son of a bitch.
quotes.speaker: Rodney Dangerfield
quotes.context: The Tonight Show with Johnny Carson, NBC, taped October 1981, during a between-jokes chat segment
quotes.fake_note: I made this one up — Dangerfield's self-deprecating marriage material and his habit of pivoting the punchline onto the questioner are pitch-perfect to his style. The giveaway is 'you nosy son of a bitch' breaking the fourth wall directly at Carson, which he'd typically redirect inward instead.

quotes.text: Look, I don't recommend my twenties to anyone. It was basically four years of bad decisions wearing a leather jacket and calling it art. But Christ, it was a good leather jacket.
quotes.speaker: John Belushi
quotes.context: Playboy magazine interview, September 1980 issue, conducted by Lawrence Linderman
quotes.fake_note: I made this one up — Belushi's self-aware, mock-philosophical deflection into a physical punchline is very consistent with how he talked in long-form interviews. The giveaway is the slight over-tidiness of the final line — Belushi was funnier when messier.

quotes.text: We finished the show in Detroit, right, and the promoter's got a cooler of Budweiser and a plate of cold cuts backstage. Cold cuts! I told him, 'Man, I just played two and a half hours — I want a hot meal and a bottle of Jack, not a goddamn deli tray.'
quotes.speaker: Freddie Mercury
quotes.context: Interview with Nick Kent for NME, published July 1977, during the News of the World tour press cycle
quotes.fake_note: I made this one up — Mercury was famously vocal about backstage riders and theatrical in his complaints, so the indignant tone rings true. The giveaway is the Jack Daniel's reference; Mercury's drink was more typically vodka and tonic, not Tennessee whiskey.

quotes.text: I showed up to the Grammy after-party still wearing my jumpsuit from the show, smelled like a goddamn brewery, and some record exec hands me a flute of champagne like I'm the Queen of fucking England. I drank it, then I drank his.
quotes.speaker: James Brown
quotes.context: Ebony magazine profile, February 1985 issue, during a roundtable on awards show culture
quotes.fake_note: I made this one up — Brown's braggadocious, self-mythologising voice and his Grammy history make this feel totally real. The giveaway is that 'Queen of fucking England' is a slightly too British simile for Brown's usual Southern-fried bravado.
